title: rubus × semicalignosus
intro: Rubus × semicalignosus, a hybrid of the wild raspberry and black raspberry, is a type of bramble that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The medicinal properties of Rubus × semicalignosus are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and salicylic acid, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × semicalignosus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, while its topical applications can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. Rubus × semicalignosus can be prepared into herbal teas, jams, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional medicine and as a dietary supplement.
